project.name: Clawvato
project.description: Business operating system for solo technical founders — memory-powered AI agent across Slack, email, calendar, Drive, meetings, and Claude Code

E.name: Proactive Intelligence
E.notes: Sweep pipeline working end-to-end (130 facts stored). Sonnet extraction fixed. Context planner built but untested.

F.name: Security Hardening
F.notes: Training wheels removed. Pre-tool security checks + output sanitization remain.

G.name: GitHub + Self-Development
G.notes: Redesigned as self-development system. Three-gate safety model. Design at docs/DESIGN_TRACK_G_SELF_DEV.md.

H.name: Plugin Adapter + CC Bridge
H.notes: Memory plugin validated (10/10 tests, 7 bugs fixed). In-tree server updated with list_working_contexts + retire_memory. DESIGN_REMOTE_MEMORY_PLUGIN.md written for HTTP transport (Option 3). Next: implement HTTP transport, deploy as separate Railway service.

J.name: Hybrid Architecture
J.notes: Replaced by CC-Native Engine (Track O). Hybrid code preserved on main branch as fallback.

M.name: Background Sweeps
M.notes: Pipeline working end-to-end. 130 facts + 693 entities stored from first backfill.

N.name: Context Planner
N.notes: Superseded by CC-Native Engine — CC plans its own context natively.

O.name: CC-Native Engine
O.notes: Claude Code as core engine via Channels. Deployed on Railway. In-tree MCP server updated with list_working_contexts + retire_memory.

infrastructure.local: macOS, DATABASE_URL=... npx tsx src/cli/index.ts start
infrastructure.dataVolume: /data (Claude CLI auth + gws config + debug workspaces)
infrastructure.maxPlan: Claude Max subscription — CC-native engine costs $0
infrastructure.memoryPlugin: github.com/andrewcovato/clawvato-memory — standalone MCP server plugin (validated, 7 bugs fixed)

metrics.pluginTestsPassing: 10/10 CRUD cycle (search, store, retrieve, working context, retire)

currentSprint.name: CC-Native Engine + Shared Memory Brain
goals: CC-Native Engine via Channels ✅ (deployed on Railway, working)
Idle timeout with verified handoff protocol ✅
clawvato-memory plugin repo ✅ (validated, 7 bugs fixed, retire_memory, category normalization)
Local CC MCP config ✅ (wired to Railway Postgres, 10/10 tests)
Swap Railway to use plugin MCP server ⬜ (Option 3: HTTP transport)
currentSprint.progress: ~90% complete. Plugin validated, in-tree server updated, design spec written. Need to commit/push and implement HTTP transport.

lastSession.type: Memory plugin validation + in-tree server update + remote plugin design spec
keyDecisions: All correction flows use soft-delete (retire_memory), never hard DELETE — preserves audit trail and superseded_by chains
Plugin schema must exactly match Railway DB — 7 bugs found from mismatches
Option 3 (HTTP MCP server) is the long-term play for plugin propagation — push to repo, all instances get updates
In-tree MCP server gets list_working_contexts + retire_memory to match plugin while we migrate to Option 3
Teaching problem: CC instances need SKILL.md injected into system prompt to know about multi-instance features

immediateBacklog.title: Commit + push both repos (clawvato in-tree fixes + clawvato-memory plugin fixes)
Inject condensed SKILL.md into config/prompts/cc-native-system.md so Railway CC knows about list_working_contexts and retire_memory
Implement Option 3: add StreamableHTTPServerTransport to plugin, deploy as separate Railway service, wire all CC instances
Run cc-native engine for 24h, monitor for crashes, verify session restart + handoff

backlog.title: Remove in-tree MCP server once Railway CC uses HTTP plugin
Track G Phase 1: read-only GitHub access
Track G Phase 2: branch + PR creation (self-development, owner-gated)
Serve SKILL.md as MCP resource from plugin server
SessionEnd hook for memory extraction safety net
Track G Phase 3: self-deploy with health check + auto-rollback

thingsNotToRedo: DON'T REBUILD WHAT COMES NATIVELY WITH CLAUDE CODE
Don't hardcode prompts in TypeScript — all prompts in config/prompts/*.md
Don't hardcode tunables in TypeScript — all operational values in config/default.json
Always take the pain now — build for durability, no short-term hacks
Always build for the future — adopt Anthropic tech as it ships
Bug reports → investigate + plan + confirm before coding
Plan before coding — don't jump straight to implementation
Don't build bespoke tools/tables for specific entity types — unified memory handles it
Store what+why, never how — implementation details don't belong in memory
MCP stdio servers MUST redirect ALL logging to stderr
Plugin schema must exactly match Railway DB — test against real DB, not assumptions
Always use soft-delete (valid_until = NOW()) for memories, never hard DELETE
Hard DELETE can dangle superseded_by FK references
CC instances won't know about MCP tools unless system prompt teaches them
Global ~/.claude/.mcp.json may not load for a specific project — use project-scoped config
Haiku max output 8192 tokens — use Sonnet for extraction
Zod schema defaults override config/default.json — must update BOTH
